full_text_rationale: Full text describes EffiSkill mining reusable optimization skills from slow/fast program pairs, storing them in a portable skill library, and retrieving/applying them in LLM-based code-efficiency optimization agents on unseen programs.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 009; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Zimu Wang, Yuling Shi, Mengfan Li, Zijun Liu, Jie M. Zhang, Chengcheng Wan, Xiaodong Gu
titulo: EffiSkill: Agent Skill Based Automated Code Efficiency Optimization
formato_armazenamento: Portable skill library mined offline from 900 Python and 900 C++ slow/fast program pairs; stores Operator Skill Cards with transformation mechanisms and Meta Skill Cards with procedural guidance for selecting and composing mechanisms.
framework_agente: EffiSkill two-stage framework with offline skill mining and execution-free skill-guided optimization
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; ablation; task-level comparison; skill-library analysis; case study
benchmarks_utilizados: EffiBench-X Python and C++ subsets from 623 optimization tasks
metricas_desempenho: OPT@1, OPT@8, paired bootstrap significance, task win/tie/loss counts, no-improvement distribution, runtime comparison, skill-family usage distribution
metricas_cl: Skill usage distribution, operator-skill family summary, recurring skill bundles, candidate-skill pairs, problem-bundle observations
baseline_comparado: Instruction, RAG, CoT, SBLLM, FasterPy, EffiCoder, ablations without retrieval, with random skills, and without multi-plan composition
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: The mined library contains 29 operator skills; the top five account for about 68.0-70.0% of usage. Mining corpus has 900 Python and 900 C++ slow/fast pairs with speedup ratio at least 2.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; all methods use a fixed candidate budget of k=8 candidates per task and execution-free inference.
resultado_principal: EffiSkill achieves the best OPT@1 and OPT@8 across GPT-5-mini and Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct in Python and C++. It improves over the strongest non-EffiSkill baseline by 12.03 points on C++ and 4.98 on Python with GPT-5-mini, and by 12.36 and 8.67 points with Qwen3-Coder.
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5-mini, EffiSkill reaches Python OPT@1/OPT@8 of 26.48/37.40 and C++ OPT@1/OPT@8 of 44.62/66.77. With Qwen3-Coder, it reaches Python 21.35/36.60 and C++ 34.19/56.50, outperforming Instruction, RAG, CoT, SBLLM, FasterPy, and EffiCoder where applicable.
custo_vs_baseline: All methods use the same k=8 candidate budget; EffiSkill is execution-free during generation and does not rely on online runtime feedback inside the optimization loop.
limitacoes: Evaluation is on competitive-programming efficiency tasks; real-world software systems may involve libraries, compiler behavior, hardware variation, and cross-file context; broader validation across models, languages, and domains is still needed.
ameacas_validade: Skill quality depends on LLM-generated traces, clustering choices, filtering thresholds, candidate budget, ranking setup, execution environment, and possible distributional similarity between mining and evaluation corpora despite direct-overlap checks.
